zh.Rougham, Mitford, Norfolk, England: 英國英格蘭諾福爾可盧加姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Rougham於大約1316年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可盧加姆時，他的父親John de Rougham42歲，母親Mrs. Alice de Rougham32歲。他於1328年與Mrs. Richard de Rougham結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.Rougham, Mitford, Norfolk, England: 英国英格兰諾福爾可盧加姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Rougham于大约1316年出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可盧加姆时，他的父亲John de Rougham42岁，母亲Mrs. Alice de Rougham32岁。他于1328年与Mrs. Richard de Rougham结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Rougham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Rougham (Norfolk, Suffolk).

variants.name: Roughan
meanings: 1 Irish: (i) from Ó Ruadhacháin, ‘son of Ruadhachán’, a personal name from a diminutive of ruadh ‘red’ (also used as a place-name and anglicized as Roughan). Compare also Rogan ( Ó Ruadhagáin), which may be a variant, both it and Ó Ruadhacháin being found in Ulster and Connacht. (ii) from Ó Robhacháin, name of a scattered ecclesiastical family, found in Clare in the 16th and 17th centuries. The personal name was earlier Reabhachán ‘skilful, artful’. See also Rohan .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Rougham was born about 1316, in Rougham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, John de Rougham, was 42 and his mother, Mrs. Alice de Rougham, was 32. He married Mrs. Richard de Rougham in 1328. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Rougham est né environ 1316, à Rougham, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de Rougham, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Alice de Rougham, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Mrs. Richard de Rougham en 1328. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Rougham um 1316 in Rougham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de Rougham 42 und seine Mutter Mrs. Alice de Rougham 32. Er hat 1328 Mrs. Richard de Rougham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Rougham è nato il circa 1316 nel Rougham, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John de Rougham, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Alice de Rougham, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Mrs. Richard de Rougham nel 1328. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.Rougham, Mitford, Norfolk, England: イギリスイギリスノーフォルクロウアム

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Rougham(이)가 대략 1316년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 루그함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Rougham(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Alice de Rougham(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1328년 Mrs. Richard de Rougham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Rougham nasceu aproximadamente 1316, em Rougham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John de Rougham, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Alice de Rougham, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Mrs. Richard de Rougham em 1328. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Rougham родился около 1316 г. в местности роугам, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John de Rougham было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Alice de Rougham, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. Richard de Rougham в 1328 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Rougham nació aproximadamente 1316, en Rougham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John de Rougham, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Mrs. Alice de Rougham, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Mrs. Richard de Rougham en 1328. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
